Commission Fabrik
A Short Film · InKenya · World Cotton Day 2026 · Tashkent

FABRIK & Family

From red earth to the moment someone puts on a piece of clothing and feels exactly like themselves.

Scroll
The Film

What Fabrik
is really about

On the surface — a cotton boll's journey from a farm in Kenya's Rift Valley to the moment she is worn by someone on the most important day of their life.

Underneath — the courage it takes to be transformed. To go through the machine, to be cut and remade, and trust that what comes out the other side is more fully you, not less.

15 to 20 minutes. Minimal dialogue. The journey told through image and music. The visual world drawn from East African woodblock print tradition — sepia, flat, grain-textured. Like something found in a market and kept for a long time.

It is not a film people watch. It is a film people carry with them.

Four of ten original panels · Full visual deck available on request

The Journey

From earth
to everything

Every stage of the supply chain. Every transformation. Every human hand that carries the dream forward — without knowing where it will finally arrive.

01
The Farm
Dreaming

Red earth. The Rift Valley. Fabrik watching the road every morning of her life. Baba reading the sky. Furaha counting her children like a prayer.

02
The Gin
Becoming

Taken apart. Terror in the enormous machine — then a single shaft of gold light. Wonder instead.

03
The Mill
Strengthening

Spun into thread. Woven together. Tension gives them strength they could not find alone.

04
The Studio
Trusting

Amara. The chalk lines. The scissors. Being found rather than cut.

05
The Wedding
Home

Worn. Needed. The whole family in one room — on different people who will never know they share an origin.

"

You don't need to see the whole of someone to know they are good. You feel it in how they move through your world.

Fabrik · On the harvest

Work With Us

Commission Fabrik

Endorsed by ICAC for World Cotton Day 2026

Three ways to bring Fabrik into your campaign, your conference, or your cause. IP remains with InKenya at every tier. What you receive is a licence to use a story that does in five minutes what most content cannot do in fifty pages.

Tier One
The Digital Narrative
$7,500
Delivery · 3 to 4 weeks
What You Receive
Full licence of all 10 Fabrik & Family panels for global campaign use
High-end 60-second parallax motion edit for social media
Optimised for Instagram, LinkedIn, and TikTok
Narrative adapted to your voice and mandate
Digital Story Partner credit
Ideal For

Campaign launches. Annual reports. Social media storytelling. Conference presentations. Advocacy materials. Immediate global deployment.

Tier Two
The Hero Film ICAC Premiere
$18,500
Delivery · 10 to 14 weeks
What You Receive
Cinematic 90-second 2D/3D hybrid animated short
Professional voiceover, sound design, and original music
Premiere screening at WCD Plenary — Tashkent, Uzbekistan
Your focus area woven into the story at script stage
Your branding in opening and closing credits
Full licence across all platforms for 24 months
Founding Commissioner credit
Ideal For

World Cotton Day Tashkent 2026. Annual conferences. Campaign launches requiring a cinematic centrepiece that travels far beyond the industry.

Tier Three
The Global Impact Campaign
$50,000
Delivery · By arrangement
What You Receive
Everything in Tier Two — The Hero Film
5-part Social Storytelling Kit — individual farmer stories
Translation into 3 languages — English, French, Uzbek
Exclusive Presenting Sponsor credit on all global distribution
Centering the voices and dreams of 28 million cotton farmers
Creator keynote at your flagship event
Ideal For

Organisations who want the full suite. A film. A campaign. Five farmer stories. Three languages. Global reach. Presenting Sponsor of the most important piece of cotton storytelling ever made.

Fixed investment tiers. No hidden costs. Every commission begins with a 30-minute briefing call to ensure the work lands exactly right for your audience and goals. IP remains with InKenya. You receive a full licence for the uses described.

Look down. What are you wearing right now?

That garment has a story.
We just want to finally tell it.
